context.description: Implement Stripe frontend integration: update PricingPage.tsx with real Stripe checkout, create SubscriptionPage.tsx for subscription management, add subscription_tier to auth context for conditional feature gating.
acceptance_criteria: frontend/src/pages/PricingPage.tsx has working Stripe checkout button that calls POST /api/v1/payments/checkout and redirects to Stripe-hosted checkout page
frontend/src/pages/SubscriptionPage.tsx shows: current subscription tier, billing date (if premium), cancel subscription button (calls DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/cancel)
frontend/src/hooks/use-auth.tsx user state includes subscription_tier field (fetched from GET /api/v1/subscriptions/status on login)

PricingPage shows 3 purchase options: (1) Free tier features list, (2) Single letter $8 checkout button (calls /payments/checkout), (3) Bundle 3-letter $24 checkout button (calls /payments/bundle-checkout, highlighted as 'Best Value')
Success/cancel redirect pages handle Stripe checkout completion
Verify: Click pricing button → Stripe checkout page loads with correct price; bundle button shows $24 for 3 letters
implementation_approach: Step 1: Read frontend/src/hooks/use-auth.tsx and auth-context.tsx to understand user state shape
Step 2: Read frontend/src/services/api.ts for API call patterns
Step 3: Add Stripe packages to package.json
Step 4: Update use-auth.tsx to fetch subscription_tier on session change
Step 5: Update PricingPage.tsx - add handleCheckout async function calling /api/v1/payments/checkout then window.location.href = session.url
Step 6: Create SubscriptionPage.tsx as a new page
Step 7: Add /subscription route to App.tsx protected routes
pre_analysis: Read frontend/src/hooks/use-auth.tsx - current user state shape and Supabase session pattern
Read frontend/src/pages/PricingPage.tsx - current pricing page structure
Read frontend/src/services/api.ts - existing API call patterns
